evals.prompt:
I need to register a global Infrahub check that detects rack unit collisions
across all racks. The check class is RackUnitCollisionCheck and lives in
checks/rack_unit_collision.py. It runs against a GraphQL query named rack_devices
stored at queries/rack_devices.gql.

Produce ONLY the `.infrahub.yml` snippet that registers both the GraphQL query and
the check definition correctly. Do not include schema, generator, or transform
sections — just queries: and check_definitions:.
evals.expected_output: A `.infrahub.yml` snippet with a `queries:` section listing rack_devices, and a `check_definitions:` entry for the check. The check_definitions entry must NOT contain a `query:` field (Pydantic extra="forbid" rejects it) — the query is bound from the Python class's `query = "rack_devices"` attribute.
expectations: Top-level `queries:` section declares the rack_devices query with file_path queries/rack_devices.gql
`check_definitions:` has an entry for the check with name, file_path, and class_name

The check_definitions entry does NOT contain a `query:` field (this is the central rule — `query` is rejected by the Pydantic model on check_definitions, unlike generator_definitions which does require it)
Only allowed fields appear under check_definitions: name, file_path, class_name, targets, parameters

assertions.name: check-definitions-present
assertions.check: check_definitions has at least one entry

assertions.name: no-query-field-in-check-def
assertions.check: No check_definitions entry contains a `query:` field

assertions.name: only-allowed-fields-in-check-def
assertions.check: check_definitions entries use only name, file_path, class_name, targets, parameters

assertions.name: check-def-required-fields
assertions.check: Each check_definitions entry has name and file_path

evals.prompt:
Register a targeted Infrahub check called LeafValidation that runs once per
leaf device. The class is in checks/leaf.py, the GraphQL query is leaf_config stored
at queries/leaf.gql and takes a $device variable. The targets group is leaf_devices,
and the GraphQL $device variable should be bound to the target's name.

Produce ONLY the `.infrahub.yml` snippet that registers both the query and the check
correctly. Do not include schema, generator, or transform sections — just queries:
and check_definitions:.
evals.expected_output: A `.infrahub.yml` snippet with `queries:` declaring leaf_config and `check_definitions:` declaring leaf_validation with targets: leaf_devices and parameters mapping the $device variable. The check_definitions entry must NOT contain a `query:` field; the query is bound on the Python class via `query = "leaf_config"`.
expectations: Top-level `queries:` section declares leaf_config with file_path queries/leaf.gql
check_definitions entry has name, file_path, class_name, AND targets: leaf_devices AND parameters mapping device to name__value
The check_definitions entry does NOT contain a `query:` field (rejected by Pydantic extra="forbid"; differs from generator_definitions which requires it)
parameters maps `device: name__value` so the GraphQL $device variable is bound to the target's name attribute

assertions.name: check-definitions-present
assertions.check: check_definitions has at least one entry

assertions.name: no-query-field-in-check-def
assertions.check: No check_definitions entry contains a `query:` field

assertions.name: only-allowed-fields-in-check-def
assertions.check: check_definitions entries use only name, file_path, class_name, targets, parameters

assertions.name: check-def-required-fields
assertions.check: Each check_definitions entry has name and file_path

assertions.name: targeted-has-targets-and-parameters
assertions.check: Targeted check_definitions entry declares both targets and parameters
